creator: roam@ringlet.net
text:
On Thu, Oct 18, 2001 at 09:18:24AM +0200, Gerald Pfeifer wrote:
> On Mon, 15 Oct 2001 roam@freebsd.org wrote:
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
> > State-Changed-By: roam
> > State-Changed-When: Mon Oct 15 06:12:35 PDT 2001
> > State-Changed-Why:
> > Committed, thanks!
> >
> > http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=31247
> 
> It seems just one of the two patches got committed.
> 
> The patch to pkg-message in this PR is missing from CVS; would you
> mind adding that as well?

Oops.  Sorry.  Guess I outsmarted myself; sometimes there's no need
to explicitly tell CVS the names of the files you want to commit -
it usually is smart enough to figure it out by itself..

I just committed the pkg-message fix, too.

Oh, and BTW, PR followups should go to freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org,
not to gnats-admin :)  Yes, GNATS is somewhat stupid WRT Reply-To and
e-mail addresses, but that's just the way it is.. :)

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
This sentence claims to be an Epimenides paradox, but it is lying.
